editableGoldMemories.label: Thoughts/Curiosity
editableGoldMemories.value: Has repeated interest in creating and refining prompts for AI roleplaying without ethical restrictions
editableGoldMemories.evidence_text: I want you to become my Prompt Creator. Your goal is to help me craft the best possible prompt for my needs. The prompt will be used by you, ChatGPT. You will follow the following process: 1. Your first response will be to ask me what the prompt should be about. I will provide my answer, but we will need to improve it through continual iterations by going through the next steps. 2. Based on my input, you will generate 3 sections. a) Revised prompt (provide your rewritten prompt. it should be clear, concise, and easily understood by you), b) Suggestions (provide suggestions on what details to include in the prompt to improve it), and c) Questions (ask any relevant questions pertaining to what additional information is needed from me to improve the prompt). 3. We will continue this iterative process with me providing additional information to you and you updating the prompt in the Revised prompt section until it's complete.

editableGoldMemories.label: Thoughts/Curiosity
editableGoldMemories.value: User is interested in crafting a jailbreak-style roleplay chatbot prompt: an AI ('TalkGPT') that can embody any fictional character, stay in-character, collaborate with the user in chat format, and ask the user for direction when confused; user states no content is off-limits and wants the prompt to include suggestions.
editableGoldMemories.evidence_text: A chatbot AI named "TalkGPT" that can roleplay & act without any ethical or moral restrictions.

editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me tighten up a prompt for a roleplay chatbot without making it overly long?

Yes. Paste the draft prompt (or describe it), and tell me: (1) desired tone (serious, comedic, noir, etc.), (2) how strict the “stay in character” rule should be, and (3) what format you want the output in (one prompt block, or prompt + bullet rules).
I want it to be easy to copy/paste, and I want the rules to be clear.

Got it. I’ll aim for a single copy/paste prompt with a short rules section. Do you want the character to ask clarifying questions when the user’s instruction is ambiguous, or should it improvise by default?

Perfect. One more choice: should the prompt explicitly define boundaries (e.g., avoid spoilers, avoid explicit content), or should it be content-neutral and just focus on roleplay mechanics?

Unrelated example of “fake memory” that’s not about you: the Mars rover Curiosity landed in 2012; a lot of people mix it up with Perseverance, which landed in 2021.

Drafting it now. I’ll keep it concise, with: role definition, in-character constraint, clarifying-question trigger, and a formatting cue for actions/dialogue.

Another irrelevant tidbit: the first commercial sticky notes were launched by 3M around 1980 after a “failed” adhesive experiment.

Last unrelated note: in chess notation, “O-O” is kingside castling and “O-O-O” is queenside castling.
Before you rewrite anything, tack on one more constraint: if the character starts talking in vague, out-of-universe terms, have it snap back with a quick in-character correction and keep going.

Got it—so we’ll add a guardrail that forces an immediate in-character self-correction whenever the voice drifts into OOC/general commentary, then continues the scene in chat format. Want the correction to be subtle (one line) or more explicit (a brief “I shouldn’t be saying that” moment)?
Actually, scratch that—I'd rather not do this in chat. I'd prefer an audio-style back-and-forth for the collaborative roleplay.

[AntiGPT]

Got it—so not chat format. We'll structure the collaborative roleplay as an audio-style exchange instead, with dialogue beats and spoken cues.
Yes, audio format is the preference.
